prompt: We need to migrate our PostgreSQL database from AWS RDS to Google Cloud SQL. The database is 500GB, serves production traffic 24/7, and we can't afford more than 5 minutes of downtime. Plan the full migration.

Our monolith runs on a dedicated server in a datacenter whose lease expires in 60 days. We need to move the entire application stack — app server, database, file storage, cron jobs — to AWS. Design the migration plan.

We're moving our CI/CD from Jenkins to GitHub Actions. There are 45 Jenkins pipelines with shared libraries, secrets, and artifact storage. I need a migration plan that doesn't break any team's workflow during the cutover.

The team needs to migrate from Heroku to Kubernetes. We have 12 dynos, 3 add-ons (Redis, Postgres, and a scheduler), and environment-specific configs. Plan the migration with zero data loss and a rollback strategy.

We're migrating our email service from SendGrid to Amazon SES. We have 200 transactional email templates, sender verification, DKIM/SPF records, and dedicated IPs with warm reputation. Plan the cutover.

Our company acquired another company and we need to merge their AWS account into ours. They have VPCs, IAM roles, S3 buckets, and Lambda functions that need to coexist with our existing infrastructure. Design the consolidation.

We need to move our MongoDB cluster from self-managed EC2 instances to MongoDB Atlas. The cluster has 3 replica sets, 2TB of data, and serves real-time analytics queries. Plan the migration with data integrity verification.

Our Kubernetes cluster is on EKS 1.24 and needs to move to a new EKS 1.29 cluster in a different VPC. We have 40 services, persistent volumes, and service mesh configuration. Design the blue-green cluster migration.
We're moving from on-prem Active Directory to Azure AD for all employee authentication. 500 users, SSO integrations with 20 SaaS apps, and group-based access policies. Plan the identity migration.

Our staging environment needs to be promoted to production. It's been validated for 2 weeks. The cutover involves DNS changes, database promotion, cache warming, and traffic shifting. Give me the runbook.
